steps.title: Use the area formula
steps.content: For a rectangle, area equals length times width. The area is $60$ square inches, and the longest side is $12$ inches, so the unknown shorter side can be represented by $w$.

steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content:
Use the rectangle area relationship with the known side length:
$$60=12w$$
This equation says the known side times the unknown side gives the given area.

steps.title: Solve for the shorter side
steps.content: Divide both sides by $12$: $$w=\frac{60}{12}=5$$ Since $5<12$, this is the shortest side of the rectangle. The answer is A.
